summary: Jest snapshot tests failed because parser error messages changed (dynamic import / TypeScript parse errors), causing snapshot mismatches in multiple test suites.
root_cause: Snapshot mismatches due to changed parser/parse error messages (e.g. for `import()` and TypeScript constructor/declare errors) — the received error text no longer matches stored snapshots.

evidence.reason: Shows a failing test suite (snapshot tests under js/_errors_/import).
The stored snapshot expected an "Unexpected token" message.

evidence.quote: + `import()` requires exactly one or two arguments. (1:1)
evidence.reason: The actual received error message is different (`import()` requires exactly one or two arguments), which caused the snapshot mismatch.

evidence.quote: SyntaxError: A parameter initializer is only allowed in a function or constructor implementation. (3:17)
evidence.reason: A TypeScript parsing error appears in the failing TypeScript test, indicating parser-produced error text that may differ from snapshot expectations.
Stack shows the error originates from the Babel parse error creation path, implicating parser error-message output as the source of changed text.

evidence.quote:
Snapshot Summary
 › 13 snapshots failed from 2 test suites. Inspect your code changes or re-run jest with `-u` to update them.
evidence.reason: Jest reports snapshot failures and suggests re-running jest with -u to update snapshots, confirming the nature of the failure.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 3 failed, 1500 passed, 1503 total
Tests:       14 failed, 5 skipped, 29634 passed, 29653 total
Snapshots:   13 failed, 12086 passed, 12099 total
evidence.reason: Quantifies the failure as multiple snapshot/test failures among many passing tests — indicating a localized but concrete regression in error text/output.

suggested_fix: If the changed error messages are intentional, update the snapshots (per the log suggestion) by re-running tests with snapshot update, e.g. `yarn test -u` or `jest -u`. If the change is not intentional, inspect recent changes to the JS/TS parser (see src/language-js/parse/babel.js and create-babel-parse-error.js in the stack) and revert or adjust the parser so error messages match the expected snapshots, then re-run tests.
